eros: noun. A winged figure of a child representing love or its power.
erose: adjective. Irregularly notched, eaten away, as though bitten.
ers: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Ersu.
expo: noun. An exposition (type of public exhibition).
expose: verb. To reveal, uncover, make visible, bring to light, introduce (to).
exposure: noun. The condition of being exposed, uncovered, or unprotected.
ope: symbol. Former ISO 639-3 language code for Old Persian.
opes: verb. third-person singular simple present indicative of ope
opus: noun. A work of music or set of works with a specified rank in an ordering of a composer's complete published works.
ore: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Orejón.
ors: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Orang Seletar.
pee: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Taje.
peer: verb. To look with difficulty, or as if searching for something.
per: symbol. ISO 639-2 /B language code for Persian.
perse: noun. A very dark (almost black) purple or blue-gray colour.
peruse: verb. To examine or consider with care.
pes: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Iranian Persian.
peso: noun. A former unit of currency in Spain and Spain's colonies, worth 8 reales; the Spanish dollar.
pore: noun. A tiny opening in the skin.
pose: noun. Common cold, head cold; catarrh.
poser: noun. A particularly difficult question or puzzle.
poseur: noun. One who affects some behaviour, style, attitude or other condition, often to impress or influence others.
pour: verb. To cause (liquid, or liquid-like substance) to flow in a stream, either out of a container or into it.
pours: verb. third-person singular simple present indicative of pour
pox: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Polabian.
pree: verb. to heed, to take notice of or pay attention to
prees: verb. third-person singular simple present indicative of pree
prex: noun. A president, especially of a university.
prose: noun. Language, particularly written language, not intended as poetry.
pur: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Puruborá.
pure: adjective. Free of flaws or imperfections; unsullied.
puree: noun. A food that has been ground or crushed into a thick liquid or paste.
purse: noun. A small bag for carrying money.
pus: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Pashto.
ree: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Rejang Kayan.
repo: noun. Clipping of repossession, most commonly of a vehicle, house or condominium.
repose: verb. To lay (someone, or part of their body) down to rest.
rex: noun. A king, particularly in ancient Rome.
rope: noun. Thick strings, yarn, monofilaments, metal wires, or strands of other cordage that are twisted together to form a stronger line.
rose: noun. A shrub of the genus Rosa, with red, pink, white or yellow flowers.
roux: noun. A mixture of fat (usually butter) and flour used to thicken sauces and stews.
rue: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Carpathian Rusyn.
rupee: noun. Any of the monetary currencies used in modern India, Mauritius, Nepal, Pakistan, the Seychelles, or Sri Lanka.
ruse: noun. A turning or doubling back, especially of animals to get out of the way of hunting dogs.
see: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Seneca.
seep: verb. To ooze or pass slowly through pores or other small openings, and in overly small quantities; said of liquids, etc.
seer: noun. One who foretells the future; a clairvoyant, prophet, soothsayer or diviner.
ser: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Serrano.
sex: noun. A category into which sexually - reproducing organisms are divided on the basis of their reproductive roles in their species; the
sop: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Songe.
sore: adjective. Causing pain or discomfort; painfully sensitive.
sou: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Southern Thai.
spore: noun. A reproductive particle, usually a single cell, released by a fungus, alga, or plant that may germinate into another.
sprue: noun. A tropical disease causing a sore throat and tongue, and disturbed digestion; psilosis.
spur: noun. A rigid implement, often roughly y-shaped, that is fixed to one's heel for the purpose of prodding a horse.
supe: noun. Clipping of supernumerary (“an extra or walk-on”).
sure: adjective. Physically secure and certain, non-failing, reliable.
user: noun. One who uses or makes use of something, a consumer or client or an express or implied licensee (free user) or a trespasser.
